title: actlint severity policy
description: The published, reviewable policy that turns a verdict, a rule class, and a confidence into a severity. Pure data: severity is computed by the engine from this table, never hand-assigned per finding, so 'why is this critical?' is always answerable from the policy. A change here is a reviewable data diff.

version.description: Semver of the severity policy — versioned alongside the crosswalk.

byVerdict.description: The base severity for a verdict-bearing (honesty) finding. under-declared is critical: an explicit false claim that removes a spec-default safety prompt is the most actionable finding the tool emits.

ruleClass.description: Severity floor by rule class. Advisory rules have no honesty verdict; they get their own floor and are never gating by default.

ruleFloor.description: Per-RuleId severity floors, applied after confidence adjustment. Keys are stable, kebab-case RuleIds.

confidenceAdjust.description: How many rungs to step an honesty finding's severity DOWN the ladder for a given confidence. Negative steps down; never suppresses. This is how 'uncertain is a first-class result' avoids both crying wolf and hiding a real risk.
